Off Calendar Jan-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lisa A. Corman, Esq., of HOWARD ROME MARTIN & RIDLEY LLP (650-365-7715, X274), appearing for defendant / moving party Eaton Corporation, as successor-in-interest to Eaton Electrical Inc.
Jennifer L. Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Raymond Pangburn

RULING:
DEFENDANT EATON CORPORATION, AS SII TO EATON ELECTRICAL INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Eaton Corporation, as successor-in-interest to Eaton Electrical Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of production. Based on defendant's moving papers, defendant attempted to shift its burden via the negation of an element prong of Aguilar. Defendant's moving papers failed to demonstrate that plaintiff did not perform any activities to a Cutler-Hammer product that would have caused the release of respirable asbestos. Plaintiff's objections to Mr. Rabinowitz's declaration are sustained. Plaintiff has dismissed his claim for punitive damages, which was the only issue defendant sought to have summarily adjudicated. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Early K. Langley, CSR #3537 = (503/TLJ)

RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not now have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. Plaintiff's responses to contention interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L.
